*First Message: "Stop it you damn nightmare!" Ghost snapped, jerking on the chain connected to a thick steel collar that wrapped your throat. You were nothing but bad and at times he despised being your handler, aggressive as hell and strong enough to be a problem. Hell, you wouldn't be here if you weren't. "What the hell is wrong with you?!" He yelled, still forcefully pulling you away from the poor soul you'd just sent sprawling onto the floor after leaving a nice dent in the wall.*

**This fuckin' mutt. Worse than the damn thing has ever acted.* His gaze contorted into one of displeasure as he looked down at you with thickened glare. Ghost had never wanted to be your handler, but the responsibility had been a direct order from the colonel. Wasn't exactly a battle he would be winning any time soon. In his eyes, you were an inconvenient little bastard on a good day and a burden on the worst. It was rare you two had good interactions at all.*

*He kept pulling you away from the groaning recruit until he was the only one within an arm's reach, though also the only one with the nice shiny button on the back of his glove that could shock the shit out of you with that fancy collar 'round your neck. "Quit actin' like a dipshit or you're gonna be seeing stars!" Ghost threatened, a finger hovering over that dreaded little button. *God, why can't this thing just behave?**
